subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Einstein's General Relativity and Astronomy
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Einstein's General Relativity and Astronomy.

hook.text: In 1915, Einstein replaced Newton's gravity with a radical idea: mass curves spacetime, and objects follow the curves.

buildup.text: His theory predicted light bending around the Sun. Arthur Eddington confirmed it during the 1919 solar eclipse.

discovery.text: Eddington's result made Einstein a global celebrity overnight. Headlines declared: 'Newton overthrown.'

twist.text: General relativity also predicted black holes, gravitational waves, and the expanding universe—all confirmed decades later.

climax.text: Modern astronomy depends on Einstein's equations: GPS, gravitational lensing, and cosmology all rely on curved spacetime.

punchline.text: Gravity isn't a force. It's curved spacetime. Einstein rewrote the cosmos.

quiz.question: How was Einstein's general relativity first confirmed observationally?
options: Light from stars was observed bending around the Sun during the 1919 solar eclipse
Gravitational waves were detected by LIGO in 2015
The expansion of the universe was measured by Hubble in 1929
